There are tons of different edibles here located at Wekiva Park. Lots of concrete trails, as well as dirt trails to walk through with many different plant types. I've found Greenbriar or Smilax, Purslane, Betony Root, Peppergrass and different species of Wood

Low-growing blueberry native to Florida. Fruiting started in April, beginning to ripen in May. Small but delicious! This patch is directly next to the hiking tail, west side. Within Wekiwa Springs State Park, entry fee required. Access: Public.
